result.summary: Adobe exposes HTTP APIs including AEM OpenAPI-based APIs, Adobe Experience Platform APIs, and Adobe Commerce REST APIs; recorded auth uses Adobe Developer Console OAuth bearer tokens for AEM, bearer token + `x-api-key` + `x-gw-ims-org-id` for Experience Platform, and Commerce REST auth remains undetermined from the recorded docs.

adobe_devconsole_oauth.label: Adobe Developer Console OAuth credentials
adobe_devconsole_oauth.setup: Create a project in [Adobe Developer Console](https://developer.adobe.com/developer-console/) and add the relevant API or service. For AEM OpenAPI-based APIs, Adobe documents OAuth Server-to-Server, OAuth Web App, and OAuth Single Page App credentials configured through Console projects in the [OpenAPI-Based APIs overview](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/experience-manager-cloud-service/content/implementing/developing/open-api-based-apis). Use the Console-generated credentials to obtain a bearer token for API calls.

adobe_api_key.label: Adobe Developer Console API key (client ID)
adobe_api_key.setup: Create a project in [Adobe Developer Console](https://developer.adobe.com/developer-console/) and add the target Adobe API. Adobe Experience Platform and related APIs require the project's client ID as the `x-api-key` header; Adobe also refers to this as the API key in pages such as the [Data Access API reference](https://developer.adobe.com/experience-platform-apis/references/data-access).

adobe_ims_org_id.label: Adobe IMS organization ID
adobe_ims_org_id.setup: Use the Adobe organization associated with your [Adobe Developer Console](https://developer.adobe.com/developer-console/) project. Experience Platform API docs state requests require the organization ID in the `x-gw-ims-org-id` header, and note it can be copied from your Experience Platform interface or project context on pages like the [Data Access API reference](https://developer.adobe.com/experience-platform-apis/references/data-access).

aio_cli_login.label: Adobe I/O CLI login session
aio_cli_login.setup: Install the Adobe I/O CLI and sign in with the CLI's login flow, which uses your Adobe account and Console access. Adobe's CLI docs are under [App Builder CLI usage](https://developer.adobe.com/app-builder/docs/guides/runtime_guides/reference_docs/cli-use) and [Events Plugin for Adobe I/O CLI](https://developer.adobe.com/events/docs/guides/cli/).

variables.name: aem_environment_url
variables.resolveFrom: your AEM as a Cloud Service environment
variables.description: Tenant/environment-specific AEM base URL; AEM docs describe registering the client ID with specific author/publish/preview tiers.

variables.name: commerce_base_url
variables.description: Commerce REST requests are made against your own Adobe Commerce / Magento instance.
